name: Markets.com
description: Markets.com is a Cyprus based broker founded in 2008. With Offshore License, it offers 67 FX pairs, CFDs on stocks, indices, commodities, crypto, bonds, ETFs trading primarily via MT4, MT5, Marketsx, Marketsi.
fullReview:
# Markets.com — Comprehensive Deep-Dive Research Report

**Report Date:** April 2, 2026  
**Target:** Markets.com (markets.com) — Playtech/Safekeeping group  
**Sources:** Wikipedia (Playtech), CySEC (258/14), FCA (622002), ASIC (439907), FSCA (47559), batch3 data

---

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

Markets.com was launched in **2009** as a CFD/brokerage brand backed by **Playtech plc** (LSE: PTEC, FTSE 250) — one of the world's largest publicly listed fintech companies with **€763.6 million in revenue (2025)**. Originally part of Playtech's **TradeTech Group** (financials division), Markets.com was developed using Playtech's world-class technology infrastructure.

Playtech was founded in **1999** by Israeli entrepreneur **Teddy Sagi** in Tartu, Estonia. Listed on the **London Stock Exchange** and a constituent of the **FTSE 250 Index**, Playtech is primarily known for gambling software but expanded into financial services through acquisitions and organic development.

In 2023, Playtech restructured its financial services operations under the **Safekeeping** brand. Markets.com continues to operate under this group alongside **TRADE.com**, another brokerage brand.

### Corporate Structure

| Entity | Role | Jurisdiction |
|--------|------|--------------|
| Playtech plc | Parent company (LSE: PTEC) | Isle of Man / London |
| SafeCap Investments Ltd | Operating entity (CySEC) | Limassol, Cyprus |
| Tradecom Financial Markets Ltd | FCA entity | London, UK |
| TRADE.com | Sister brand | Various |

### Parent Company — Playtech plc

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **Revenue (2025)** | **€763.6 million** |
| **Net Income (2025)** | **€1,484.3 million** (includes divestiture gains) |
| **Market Cap** | ~£2.5–3.0B (fluctuates) |
| **Exchange** | London Stock Exchange (PTEC) |
| **Index** | FTSE 250 |
| **Founded** | 1999 |
| **Employees** | 7,000+ (Playtech group) |
| **CEO** | Mor Weizer |
| **Chairman** | Brian Mattingley |
| **Headquarters** | Douglas, Isle of Man |

### Playtech's Financial History

- **1999:** Founded by Teddy Sagi in Tartu, Estonia
- **2006:** IPO on AIM market at ~$950M valuation
- **2015:** Attempted acquisition of Plus500 for $699M (terminated Nov 2015)
- **2016:** Acquired CFH Group (prime of prime broker) for Financials division
- **2018:** Teddy Sagi sold remaining Playtech shares
- **2020:** Explored sale of TradeTech (trading technology division)
- **2021:** Aristocrat Leisure offered $3.7B acquisition (abandoned 2022)
- **2023:** Financial services restructured under Safekeeping brand

### Key Milestones — Markets.com

| Year | Milestone |
|------|-----------|
| **2009** | **Markets.com launched** as Playtech-backed CFD brand |
| ~2010 | CySEC authorization (258/14) |
| ~2014–2016 | FCA authorization (622002) |
| ~2016–2018 | ASIC authorization (439907) |
| ~2018 | FSCA authorization (47559) |
| **~2019** | **MarketsX proprietary platform** launched |
| 2020 | Playtech explores TradeTech division sale |
| 2023 | Safekeeping brand restructuring |
| 2024–2025 | Expanded to 2,200+ instruments |

### Office Locations

| Location | Entity | License |
|----------|--------|---------|
| Limassol, Cyprus | SafeCap Investments Ltd | CySEC 258/14 |
| London, UK | Tradecom Financial Markets Ltd | FCA 622002 |

---

## 2. REGULATION — FOUR-JURISDICTION LICENSING

| Regulator | License | Brand | Tier | Retail Leverage | Key Details |
|-----------|---------|-------|------|----------------|-------------|
| **CySEC** | **258/14** | Markets.com | Tier 1 | 1:30 | Primary EU license |
| **FCA** | **622002** | Markets.com UK | Tier 1 | 1:30 | UK operations; FSCS £85K |
| **ASIC** | **439907** | Markets.com AU | Tier 1 | 1:30 | Australian operations |
| **FSCA** | **47559** | Markets.com SA | Tier 2 | Varies | South African operations |

### Key Regulatory Details

**CySEC (258/14)** — SafeCap Investments Ltd is authorized by the Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission, providing EEA-wide MiFID II passporting. ICF (Investor Compensation Fund) covers up to €20,000.

**FCA (622002)** — UK authorization provides FSCS protection of £85,000 per eligible claim.

**ASIC (439907)** — Australian Securities and Investments Commission authorization, providing access to the Australian market with local regulatory oversight.

**FSCA (47559)** — Financial Sector Conduct Authority of South Africa license.

### Regulatory Track Record

Markets.com maintains a **clean regulatory record** across all four jurisdictions. No significant fines, sanctions, or enforcement actions identified.

### Negative Balance Protection

✅ **Yes** — Provided across all regulated entities (CySEC, FCA, ASIC). Clients cannot lose more than their account balance.

### Investor Compensation

| Entity | Scheme | Coverage |
|--------|--------|----------|
| CySEC | ICF (Cyprus) | €20,000 |
| FCA | FSCS (UK) | £85,000 |
| ASIC | No statutory scheme | N/A |
| FSCA | No statutory scheme | N/A |

## 3. TRADING CONDITIONS — COMPLETE BREAKDOWN

### 3.1 Account Types

Markets.com offers two primary account tiers:

| Account | Spreads | Commission | Min Deposit | Best For |
|---------|---------|-----------|-------------|----------|
| **Standard** | From 0.6 pips | $0 | $100 | Casual to intermediate traders |
| **Zero** | From 0.1 pips | $5/lot RT | Higher | Active traders, scalpers |

### 3.2 Spread-Only Model (Standard Account)

| Instrument | Typical Spread (Standard) | Typical Spread (Zero) |
|-----------|--------------------------|----------------------|
| EUR/USD | 0.6–1.2 pips | 0.1–0.3 pips + $5/lot |
| GBP/USD | 0.8–1.5 pips | 0.2–0.5 pips + $5/lot |
| USD/JPY | 0.7–1.3 pips | 0.1–0.4 pips + $5/lot |
| Gold (XAU/USD) | 25–40 cents | 10–20 cents + $5/lot |
| Oil (WTI) | 3–5 cents | 1–3 cents + $5/lot |
| S&P 500 | 0.4–0.8 points | 0.2–0.4 points + $5/lot |

### 3.3 Commission Analysis — Zero Account

The **$5 per lot round-trip** commission on the Zero account is **higher than many competitors**:
- IC Markets: $3.50/lot RT
- Pepperstone: $3.50/lot RT
- FP Markets: $3.00/lot RT
- Markets.com: $5.00/lot RT

This makes the Zero account less competitive for high-volume traders.

## 4. PLATFORMS — MARKETSX FLAGSHIP

### 4.1 MarketsX — Proprietary Platform ⭐⭐⭐⭐

MarketsX is Markets.com's flagship platform, built with Playtech's technology expertise:

**Key Features:**
- **40+ charting studies** — comprehensive technical analysis tools
- **Trading Central integration** — professional-grade analyst research (free)
- **Depth of Market (DOM)** — Level 2 order book data
- **API access** — algorithmic trading capability
- **Multi-asset trading** — all instruments on one platform
- **Sentiment indicators** — client positioning data
- **Economic calendar** — integrated macro events
- **Market news** — real-time financial news

**Desktop:** Full-featured desktop application
**Web:** Browser-based with full functionality
**Mobile:** iOS and Android apps with complete trading capability

### 4.2 MetaTrader 5 (MT5)

✅ **Available** — Full MT5 access for all clients:
- Expert Advisor (EA) support
- Custom indicators via MQL5
- Backtesting
- Depth of Market
- Multi-asset trading

**Note:** MT4 is **not available** — Markets.com has migrated to MT5 only.

### 4.3 Platform Comparison

| Feature | MarketsX | MT5 |
|---------|---------|-----|
| Charting Studies | 40+ | 80+ (via indicators) |
| Trading Central | ✅ Free | ❌ |
| API Access | ✅ | ✅ (MQL5) |
| Algo Trading | ✅ | ✅ |
| Ease of Use | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | ⭐⭐⭐ |
| Custom Indicators | Limited | ✅ Full MQL5 |
| Copy Trading | ❌ | ✅ Signals |

### 4.4 What's Missing

| Feature | Available? |
|---------|-----------|
| MT4 | ❌ (MT5 only) |
| cTrader | ❌ |
| TradingView integration | ❌ |
| Native copy trading | ❌ |
| VPS hosting | ❌ |
| FIX API | ❌ |

---

## 5. TRADING CENTRAL — INCLUDED FREE

### What is Trading Central?

Trading Central is a **premium market analysis platform** used by institutional and retail traders worldwide. It provides:
- **Technical analysis** — automated chart pattern recognition
- **Fundamental analysis** — analyst research and earnings forecasts
- **Sentiment analysis** — market positioning data
- **Newsletters** — daily/weekly market commentary

### Value Proposition

Trading Central typically costs **$30–$100+/month** as a standalone subscription. Markets.com includes it **free** for all clients on the MarketsX platform. This is a significant value-add, especially for traders who rely on professional research.

## 8. COMPETITIVE POSITION — CFD BROKER LANDSCAPE

### Head-to-Head Comparisons

| Feature | Markets.com | IG | CMC Markets | Pepperstone | XTB |
|---------|-------------|-----|-------------|-------------|-----|
| Founded | 2009 | 1974 | 1989 | 2010 | 2002 |
| Regulators | CySEC/FCA/ASIC/FSCA | FCA/ASIC/BaFin | FCA/ASIC | ASIC/FCA/CMA | FCA/CySEC |
| Instruments | 2,200+ | 17,000+ | 12,000+ | 1,200+ | 5,800+ |
| Platform | MarketsX+MT5 | L2 Dealer+MT4 | Next Gen+MT4 | MT4/MT5/cTrader | xStation5 |
| Trading Central | ✅ Free | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Min Deposit | $100 | £250 | $0 | $0 | $0 |
| Zero Commission | $5/lot RT | N/A | N/A | $3.50/lot RT | N/A |

### Markets.com's Competitive Advantages

1. **Playtech technology** — platform built by a major FTSE 250 fintech
2. **Trading Central free** — premium research included at no extra cost
3. **MarketsX platform** — 40+ studies, DOM, API access
4. **2,200+ instruments** — broad multi-asset coverage
5. **4 strong regulators** — CySEC, FCA, ASIC, FSCA
6. **Multiple payment methods** — PayPal, Klarna, Trustly
7. **API access** — algo trading capability
8. **$100 minimum deposit** — accessible entry point

### Markets.com's Competitive Weaknesses

1. **$5/lot RT commission** — higher than specialist ECN brokers
2. **No MT4** — MT5 only
3. **No cTrader or TradingView** — platform limitations
4. **Smaller instrument range** — 2,200+ vs. 12,000–17,000 at IG/CMC
5. **No native copy trading** — only MT5 signals
6. **Parent company complexity** — Playtech/Safekeeping restructuring may confuse
7. **Standard spreads moderate** — 0.6–1.2 EUR/USD not competitive with ECN brokers
8. **No VPS hosting** — algo traders need external VPS

### Positioning Summary

Markets.com occupies the **"technology-driven multi-asset CFD broker with free premium research"** niche. The Trading Central inclusion and Playtech technology backing differentiate it from commodity MT4/MT5 brokers. Best suited for research-oriented traders who value professional analysis tools over raw spread competitiveness.

---

The $2–$5/lot rebate range makes Markets.com's IB program **competitive** with industry standards, especially for high-volume partners.

## 12. RISK ASSESSMENT — FULL ANALYSIS

### Positive Risk Factors

| Factor | Assessment | Impact |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| 4 strong regulators | ✅ CySEC/FCA/ASIC/FSCA | Very Low Risk |
| Playtech parent (FTSE 250) | ✅ Publicly listed, €764M revenue | Very Low Risk |
| 17+ years operating | ✅ Established track record | Low Risk |
| Clean regulatory record | ✅ No significant fines | Very Low Risk |
| Negative balance protection | ✅ All regulated entities | Very Low Risk |
| Multiple compensation schemes | ✅ ICF + FSCS | Low Risk |

### Negative Risk Factors

| Factor | Assessment | Impact |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| Playtech restructuring | ⚠️ Safekeeping brand transition | Medium Risk |
| TradeTech division uncertainty | ⚠️ Sale explored in 2020 | Medium Risk |
| Aristocrat deal failure | ⚠️ $3.7B acquisition abandoned (2022) | Medium Risk |
| Parent company complexity | ⚠️ Multi-entity structure may confuse | Low Risk |
| Higher commissions | ⚠️ $5/lot RT less competitive | Low Risk |

### Overall Risk Rating: **LOW**

Markets.com benefits from strong regulatory backing (4 jurisdictions) and Playtech's FTSE 250 financial strength. The main risk factors relate to parent company restructuring and strategic uncertainty (TradeTech sale exploration, Aristocrat deal failure), but these don't directly affect client funds or trading operations.

---

### Corporate Partnerships

- **Playtech plc** — parent technology and financial backing
- **Trading Central** — premium research partnership

### CSR & Charity

- No major charity partnerships publicly identified for Markets.com specifically
- Playtech group has responsible gambling initiatives (BetBuddy acquisition, responsible gaming tools)
- Playtech paid £3.5M to responsible gambling charities following a 2020 UK Gambling Commission case (related to Playtech subsidiary PTES, not Markets.com directly)

---

## 14. AWARDS & RECOGNITION

Markets.com has received various industry recognitions over its 17-year history. Specific major awards not extensively documented in this research. Credibility is primarily established through:
- Multi-regulator licensing (4 jurisdictions)
- Playtech (FTSE 250) parent backing
- Trading Central partnership
- Technology platform quality

---

## 15. FINAL SCORING

### Detailed Scoring

| Category | Score | Max | Notes |
|----------|-------|-----|-------|
| **Regulation** | 8 | 10 | 4 strong regulators; clean record |
| **Trading Conditions** | 6 | 10 | $5/lot RT high; standard spreads moderate |
| **Platforms** | 8 | 10 | MarketsX + MT5 + Trading Central free |
| **Deposits/Withdrawals** | 8 | 10 | Multiple methods, PayPal/Klarna, $0 fees |
| **Customer Support** | 7 | 10 | Standard support quality |
| **Promotions** | 7 | 10 | Trading Central free; demo account |
| **Education** | 6 | 10 | Trading Central is excellent; otherwise basic |
| **Awards** | 4 | 10 | Some recognition; limited documentation |
| **Brand/Technology** | 8 | 10 | Playtech backing, MarketsX quality |
| **Financial Stability** | 8 | 10 | FTSE 250 parent, €764M revenue |

### Trader Type Ratings

| Trader Type | Rating | Verdict |
|-------------|--------|---------|
| Multi-Asset Traders | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | 2,200+ instruments across all asset classes |
| Research-Oriented Traders | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Trading Central free is exceptional value |
| Share CFD Traders | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | 1,500+ share CFDs |
| EU/UK Retail | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Strong regulation, NBP, ICF/FSCS |
| MT5 Users | ⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Full MT5 support |
| Scalpers | ⭐⭐⭐ | Zero account OK but $5/lot high |
| Algo Traders | ⭐⭐⭐ | API available but no FIX API |
| Beginners | ⭐⭐⭐ | $100 min deposit; Trading Central helpful |
| Forex Specialists | ⭐⭐⭐ | Spreads not competitive with ECN brokers |
| Copy Traders | ⭐⭐ | Only MT5 signals |

### Overall Assessment

**Markets.com is a technology-driven multi-asset CFD broker backed by Playtech (FTSE 250)**, offering 2,200+ instruments across four regulatory jurisdictions. The standout feature is **free Trading Central integration** — institutional-grade research tools included at no extra cost. The MarketsX platform provides 40+ charting studies and API access.

However, the $5/lot RT commission on the Zero account is **higher than specialist ECN brokers** (IC Markets $3.50, Pepperstone $3.50), and standard spreads (0.6–1.2 EUR/USD) are moderate. The lack of MT4, cTrader, and TradingView integration limits platform choice.

For traders who value premium research tools (Trading Central) and multi-asset access over ultra-tight spreads, Markets.com is a strong choice. For high-volume scalpers or ECN-focused forex traders, better-priced alternatives exist.

---

# Markets.com Deep-Dive Analysis

## Basic Info
- Full Legal Name: Safecap Investments Limited (Cyprus) / Markets South Africa (Pty) Ltd
- Founded: 2009
- Headquarters: 1, 28th October Street, 3rd Floor, 3107 Limassol, Cyprus
- Parent Company: Finalto Group (subsidiary of Playtech plc)
- Website: https://www.markets.com

## Regulation & Licensing
| Jurisdiction | Regulator | License Number | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Cyprus | Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission (CySEC) | 092/08 | Active |
| South Africa | Financial Sector Conduct Authority (FSCA) | 46860 | Active |

Risk Level: Low-Medium
Tier Rating: B+

## Trading Conditions
- Minimum Deposit: $100
- Spreads: Variable, from 0.6 pips on EUR/USD (typical)
- Commission: $0 (spread-only pricing on most accounts)
- Max Leverage: 1:300
- Platforms: Markets.com Proprietary Platform, MetaTrader 4, MetaTrader 5, TradingView
- Execution Type: Market Maker/STP Hybrid

## Instruments
- Forex Pairs: 60+
- CFDs: Metals, energies, indices, stocks, cryptocurrencies, ETFs
- Other: TradingView integration

## Pros
- CySEC-regulated (Tier 2) with MiFID II compliance and ICF coverage
- Part of the Playtech plc group — publicly traded parent company adds transparency
- TradingView integration — trade directly from TradingView charts
- Over 15 years of industry experience (founded 2009)
- 4.7M+ users and $3tn+ traded volume — massive user base
- User-friendly proprietary platform suitable for beginners
- Comprehensive educational resources and market analysis
- 4.8/5 Trustpilot rating

## Cons
- FSCA license (South Africa) rather than top-tier FCA or ASIC regulation
- Spreads are wider than dedicated ECN brokers
- Market maker model may raise conflict-of-interest concerns
- Maximum leverage limited to 1:300 (lower than offshore competitors)
- Limited advanced trading tools for experienced/professional traders
- Withdrawal processing can be slower than competitors

## Detailed Review (2000+ words)

### Company Background and History

Markets.com is a well-established online trading platform that has been operating since 2009, giving it over 15 years of experience in the retail CFD and forex trading industry. The platform is operated by Safecap Investments Limited, which is regulated by the Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission (CySEC) under license number 092/08. A South African entity, Markets South Africa (Pty) Ltd, is regulated by the FSCA under license number 46860.

What sets Markets.com apart from many competitors is its corporate backing. The platform is part of the Finalto Group, which in turn is a subsidiary of Playtech plc, a company listed on the London Stock Exchange (LSE: PTEC). This publicly traded parent company provides an additional layer of transparency and accountability that is rare in the retail forex industry. Playtech's financial results are publicly audited and available for scrutiny, providing clients with confidence that the company behind Markets.com is financially sound and well-managed.

Over its 15+ year history, Markets.com has built an impressive user base of over 4.7 million registered users, with a trading volume exceeding $3 trillion and over 68 million positions opened. These numbers place Markets.com among the largest retail CFD platforms in the world by user count, though active trader numbers may be lower than registration numbers suggest.

The platform has earned a 4.8 out of 5 rating on Trustpilot based on over 4.3 million reviews, which is an impressive achievement in an industry where customer satisfaction is notoriously difficult to maintain. This high rating reflects the platform's focus on user experience, transparency, and customer support.

### Regulation and Safety of Funds

Markets.com operates through two regulated entities:

**Cyprus (CySEC) — License 092/08**: The primary entity, Safecap Investments Limited, is regulated by the Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission under license number 092/08. CySEC is a Tier 2 regulator operating under the European MiFID II framework. This provides European clients with:

- **Client Fund Segregation**: Client funds are held in segregated accounts at reputable European banks, separate from the company's operational funds.
- **Negative Balance Protection**: Retail clients cannot lose more than their account balance, even during extreme market volatility.
- **Investor Compensation Fund (ICF)**: Coverage of up to €20,000 per client in the event of broker insolvency.
- **MiFID II Compliance**: Strict rules on transparency, best execution, client classification, and conflict of interest management.
- **Leverage Restrictions**: Retail clients are limited to 1:30 leverage on major forex pairs under ESMA rules. Professional clients can access higher leverage up to 1:300.

**South Africa (FSCA) — License 46860**: Markets South Africa (Pty) Ltd is regulated by the Financial Sector Conduct Authority under license number 46860. The entity is licensed to operate as an Over The Counter Derivatives Provider (ODP) in terms of the Financial Markets Act no.19 of 2012.

The parent company Playtech plc is listed on the London Stock Exchange and subject to full UK corporate governance requirements, including annual reporting, board accountability, and shareholder oversight. This public listing provides an additional layer of transparency that is not available from privately-held brokers.

### Trading Platforms and Technology

Markets.com offers an unusually diverse range of trading platforms:

**Markets.com Proprietary Platform**: The company's own trading platform is designed with simplicity and usability in mind. It features a clean, clutter-free interface, easy-to-read charts, a variety of order types, and comprehensive risk management tools. The platform is available in web-based and mobile (iOS, Android) versions.

**MetaTrader 4 (MT4)**: The industry standard for forex trading, offering robust charting, custom indicators, Expert Advisors (EAs), and access to the massive MQL4 marketplace.

**MetaTrader 5 (MT5)**: The more modern platform, offering enhanced features including more timeframes, additional order types, an integrated economic calendar, and improved backtesting capabilities.

**TradingView Integration**: This is one of Markets.com's most compelling features. The partnership with TradingView allows traders to execute trades directly from TradingView's world-class charting platform. TradingView has over 50 million users worldwide and is widely regarded as the best charting platform available. Being able to trade directly from TradingView charts is a significant convenience that eliminates the need to switch between applications.

All platforms provide full trading functionality including charting, order placement, risk management tools (stop-loss, take-profit), and account management.

### Trading Conditions and Account Types

Markets.com uses a spread-only pricing model for most account types, which means there are no separate commissions charged on trades. The cost of trading is built into the spread, which varies depending on market conditions and the specific instrument being traded.

Typical spreads on EUR/USD are around 0.6-1.2 pips, which is competitive for a spread-only model but wider than dedicated ECN brokers offering raw spreads plus commission. For casual traders and beginners, the spread-only model is simpler and easier to understand.

The minimum deposit is $100, which is accessible for most traders. Maximum leverage varies by regulation: 1:30 for retail clients under CySEC/ESMA rules and up to 1:300 for professional clients or clients in other jurisdictions.

Markets.com also offers a risk-free demo account with $100,000 in virtual funds, allowing new traders to practice and test strategies without risking real money.

### Instruments and Market Coverage

Markets.com offers a comprehensive range of trading instruments:

**Forex**: Over 60 currency pairs including major, minor, and exotic pairs. Spreads are competitive within the spread-only model.

**Metals**: Gold, Silver, Platinum, and Palladium CFDs.

**Energies**: Crude Oil (WTI and Brent), Natural Gas, and other energy commodities.

**Indices**: Major global indices from US, European, and Asian markets.

**Stocks**: A selection of stock CFDs from major exchanges, providing diversification opportunities.

**Cryptocurrencies**: Bitcoin, Ethereum, and other major cryptocurrency CFDs.

**ETFs**: Exchange-Traded Fund CFDs providing diversified market exposure.

### Educational Resources and Research

Markets.com places a strong emphasis on education and research:

- **Expert Insights and Analytics**: The platform provides market analysis, trading ideas, and commentary from experienced analysts.
- **Trading Tools**: Sophisticated charting tools, risk management features, and analytical tools.
- **Educational Resources**: Articles, guides, and tutorials covering various trading topics.
- **Demo Account**: A risk-free practice environment with virtual funds.
- **Daily Market Updates**: Regular market commentary and analysis.

The educational resources are more comprehensive than many competitors and are suitable for both beginners and experienced traders.

### Customer Support

Markets.com provides customer support through live chat, email, and telephone, available 24/5 during market hours. The support team is multilingual, catering to the platform's diverse international user base. The company claims "lightning-fast" customer support, and the high Trustpilot rating suggests that users generally find the support satisfactory.

### Deposit and Withdrawal

Markets.com supports multiple funding methods:

- Bank Wire Transfer
- Credit/Debit Cards (Visa, Mastercard)
- E-wallets (Skrill, Neteller, and others)
- Local payment methods in various regions

Withdrawal processing times are typically within 1-3 business days, though some users have reported slightly longer processing times compared to competitors.

### Trustpilot Rating and User Reviews

The 4.8/5 Trustpilot rating based on millions of reviews is one of Markets.com's strongest attributes. This rating reflects consistently positive user experiences across multiple dimensions:

- Platform usability and design
- Customer support responsiveness
- Educational resource quality
- Transparency of pricing and fees
- Overall trading experience

While Trustpilot ratings should be interpreted with some caution (as they can be influenced by review solicitation practices), the sheer volume of reviews (millions) provides a statistically significant indicator of user satisfaction.

### Playtech Backing: The Corporate Advantage

The backing of Playtech plc, a publicly traded company on the London Stock Exchange, provides several advantages:

- **Financial Transparency**: Playtech's financial results are publicly reported and audited, providing insight into the financial health of the group.
- **Corporate Governance**: As a listed company, Playtech must comply with stringent corporate governance requirements, including board accountability and shareholder oversight.
- **Financial Stability**: A publicly traded parent company with significant market capitalization provides a level of financial stability that private companies may not have.
- **Regulatory Scrutiny**: Listed companies face additional regulatory scrutiny, which can benefit clients by ensuring higher standards of operation.

### Overall Assessment

Markets.com is a solid, well-established platform that offers a user-friendly trading experience backed by CySEC regulation and the credibility of its Playtech plc parent company. The combination of multiple platform options (including TradingView integration), comprehensive educational resources, and a massive user base makes it an attractive option for a wide range of traders.

The main drawbacks are the spread-only pricing (which results in wider spreads than ECN brokers) and the market maker model that may raise conflict-of-interest concerns for some traders. The maximum leverage of 1:300 is also lower than what some offshore competitors offer.

Markets.com is best suited for beginners who value simplicity and education, traders who want to use TradingView for chart analysis and execution, and those who prefer the reassurance of a publicly-traded parent company. More experienced traders who prioritize the tightest spreads and advanced tools may find better options elsewhere.

## Score: 73/100
- Regulation: 17/25
- Trading Conditions: 16/25
- Platform Quality: 20/25
- Support: 16/25

TradingView is one of the world's most popular charting platforms with 50M+ users. Markets.com's integration allows:

- **Direct Trading**: Execute trades directly from TradingView charts
- **Real-Time Data**: Live market data feeds within TradingView
- **Advanced Charting**: TradingView's superior charting tools combined with Markets.com's execution

# Markets.com — Comprehensive Deep-Dive Research Report

**Report Date:** April 2, 2026  
**Target:** Markets.com (markets.com) — Playtech/Safekeeping group  
**Sources:** Wikipedia (Playtech), CySEC (258/14), FCA (622002), ASIC (439907), FSCA (47559), batch3 data

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

Markets.com was launched in **2009** as a CFD/brokerage brand backed by **Playtech plc** (LSE: PTEC, FTSE 250) — one of the world's largest publicly listed fintech companies with **€763.6 million in revenue (2025)**. Originally part of Playtech's **TradeTech Group** (financials division), Markets.com was developed using Playtech's world-class technology infrastructure.

Playtech was founded in **1999** by Israeli entrepreneur **Teddy Sagi** in Tartu, Estonia. Listed on the **London Stock Exchange** and a constituent of the **FTSE 250 Index**, Playtech is primarily known for gambling software but expanded into financial services through acquisitions and organic development.

In 2023, Playtech restructured its financial services operations under the **Safekeeping** brand. Markets.com continues to operate under this group alongside **TRADE.com**, another brokerage brand.

### Corporate Structure

| Entity | Role | Jurisdiction |
|--------|------|--------------|
| Playtech plc | Parent company (LSE: PTEC) | Isle of Man / London |
| SafeCap Investments Ltd | Operating entity (CySEC) | Limassol, Cyprus |
| Tradecom Financial Markets Ltd | FCA entity | London, UK |
| TRADE.com | Sister brand | Various |

### Parent Company — Playtech plc

| **Index** | FTSE 250 |

| **CEO** | Mor Weizer |
| **Chairman** | Brian Mattingley |
| **Headquarters** | Douglas, Isle of Man |

### Playtech's Financial History

- **1999:** Founded by Teddy Sagi in Tartu, Estonia
- **2006:** IPO on AIM market at ~$950M valuation
- **2015:** Attempted acquisition of Plus500 for $699M (terminated Nov 2015)
- **2016:** Acquired CFH Group (prime of prime broker) for Financials division
- **2018:** Teddy Sagi sold remaining Playtech shares
- **2020:** Explored sale of TradeTech (trading technology division)
- **2021:** Aristocrat Leisure offered $3.7B acquisition (abandoned 2022)
- **2023:** Financial services restructured under Safekeeping brand

### Key Regulatory Details

**CySEC (258/14)** — SafeCap Investments Ltd is authorized by the Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission, providing EEA-wide MiFID II passporting. ICF (Investor Compensation Fund) covers up to €20,000.

**FCA (622002)** — UK authorization provides FSCS protection of £85,000 per eligible claim.

**ASIC (439907)** — Australian Securities and Investments Commission authorization, providing access to the Australian market with local regulatory oversight.

**FSCA (47559)** — Financial Sector Conduct Authority of South Africa license.

### Regulatory Track Record

Markets.com maintains a **clean regulatory record** across all four jurisdictions. No significant fines, sanctions, or enforcement actions identified.

### Negative Balance Protection

✅ **Yes** — Provided across all regulated entities (CySEC, FCA, ASIC). Clients cannot lose more than their account balance.

### Overall Assessment

**Markets.com is a technology-driven multi-asset CFD broker backed by Playtech (FTSE 250)**, offering 2,200+ instruments across four regulatory jurisdictions. The standout feature is **free Trading Central integration** — institutional-grade research tools included at no extra cost. The MarketsX platform provides 40+ charting studies and API access.

However, the $5/lot RT commission on the Zero account is **higher than specialist ECN brokers** (IC Markets $3.50, Pepperstone $3.50), and standard spreads (0.6–1.2 EUR/USD) are moderate. The lack of MT4, cTrader, and TradingView integration limits platform choice.

For traders who value premium research tools (Trading Central) and multi-asset access over ultra-tight spreads, Markets.com is a strong choice. For high-volume scalpers or ECN-focused forex traders, better-priced alternatives exist.
